_comment: STARTER TEMPLATE for ~/.octave/predictions/<MCC_ID>.json. The resonance loop copies this file to that location on first run, replaces the REPLACE_WITH_* placeholders with real values from the user's BigQuery setup, and starts appending real predictions to the empty `predictions` array. The `_example_card` field below is reference-only — the loop never reads it, it's just there to show the canonical shape of a prediction card. See skills/ads/references/prediction-cards.md for the full schema and the prediction type taxonomy.

lessons: STRUCTURAL PREDICTIONS GENERALIZE; UNIT-SPECIFIC ONES DON'T. Prefer claims about properties of the auction over claims about named units.
DON'T PREDICT REGRESSION-TO-MEAN ON UNITS BELOW THE VOLUME GATE. Use exposure-projection predictions instead — those can be confirmed even at low volume.
INCONCLUSIVE_FAVORABLE / INCONCLUSIVE_UNFAVORABLE is a real distinction. Track direction separately so the volume gate can be calibrated over time.
FIELD STABILITY IS A FIRST-CLASS CONSIDERATION. Always generate one field-stability prediction per run.

_example_card._comment: Reference-only example showing the canonical structure of a cpc-efficiency-gap prediction card (the strongest type). The loop never reads this field — it's here so a future LLM session can see the expected shape when generating new predictions. Delete this field if you find it visually distracting; the loop won't notice.
_example_card.generated_by: resonance loop run, training window YYYY-MM-DD to YYYY-MM-DD (N days)
_example_card.claim: The CPC ratio between the highest-CPC ad group and the lowest-CPC ad group (with >= 10 clicks each) will persist at >= 3x for the next 7 days
_example_card.confirms: ratio of (max ad-group CPC) / (min ad-group CPC, with >= 10 clicks each) >= 3.0 in evaluation window
_example_card.refutes: ratio < 2.0 in evaluation window, both ad groups having >= 10 clicks
_example_card.inconclusive: fewer than 2 ad groups have >= 10 clicks in the evaluation window
_example_card.evaluation_sql: -- Stats-only query, no dim joins — immune to the daily-snapshot dim-table inflation bug (Gotcha #4). SELECT MAX(cpc_usd) / NULLIF(MIN(cpc_usd), 0) AS ratio, COUNTIF(clicks >= 10) AS qualifying_ad_groups FROM (SELECT ad_group_id, ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(metrics_cost_micros) / 1000000, SUM(metrics_clicks)), 2) AS cpc_usd, SUM(metrics_clicks) AS clicks FROM `<project>.<dataset>.ads_AdGroupBasicStats_<MCC>` WHERE _DATA_DATE BETWEEN '<window_start>' AND '<window_end>' GROUP BY ad_group_id HAVING clicks >= 10)
_example_card.rationale: CPC gaps are usually structural — they reflect Quality Score / keyword-creative match rather than transient bidding noise. Predictions of this type have generalized to brand new ad groups in past backtests.
_example_card.action_if_confirmed: Promote the underlying source card hypothesis to higher confidence. Recommend pausing or reworking the high-CPC ad group.
_example_card.action_if_refuted: Investigate what changed. The CPC gap may have been a transient auction artifact rather than structural.
_example_card.action_if_inconclusive: Re-evaluate next run when more click volume has accumulated.
